summary: Added a self-contained macOS/Linux tmux cockpit (`tools/decomp_work/tmux_control/cockpit.sh` + COCKPIT_MAC.md) for driving GLM and Codex agents on the decomp pipeline, replacing an uncommitted Windows control.sh/txk.sh/psmux setup with real tmux.
decomp_lessons: TUI apps (opencode, codex) rewrite pane_title via OSC escapes, making title-based pane lookup unreliable; tag panes with a tmux pane user-option (@role) instead, which the TUI cannot overwrite.

For orchestration reliability prefer headless dispatch (`opencode run`, `codex exec`) over scripting keystrokes into an interactive TUI pane — the interactive panes are for a human at the keyboard, not for automated driving.
Idle/busy detection for a tmux pane can be approximated cheaply by capturing the pane tail twice ~1.6s apart and comparing for equality, without needing an app-specific status API.
smart_moves: Sourced API keys from an env file OUTSIDE the repo (~/.config/decomp-keys/cockpit.env, override via DECOMP_COCKPIT_ENV) so credentials are never committed.
Kept the launch command self-healing: each TUI pane's launch string ends with `exec $SHELL` so a TUI crash/exit drops back to a shell instead of destroying the pane.

key_files.role: New tmux cockpit: bootstrap/attach a 2x2 orchestrator|glm|codex|shell grid, headless ask-glm/ask-codex dispatch, idle detection, pane-role tagging.
Setup/usage docs: prerequisites, credential file layout, headless vs interactive driving modes, troubleshooting (opencode DB reset, codex login refresh).
